lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I do not speak to outlanders.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Maybe you should speak with Rellekka's Chieftain.

sections.title: After The Fremennik Isles at Rellekka

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hail the conquering hero!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heh. Thanks - I was pretty heroic even if I say so myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Well now, the history of the isles is a bloody tale. When told in its intended form it takes 9 days and 9 nights. However, I will give you the abridged version.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew!

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Four generations ago there was a mighty king who ruled wisely and was loved by all.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: He had many sons, most of whom proved themselves in the usual Fremennik ways of fighting, sailing, drinking and so on.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: His youngest two sons, however, were a problem. Ever since they were old enough to hit one another they had bickered and fought.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Their names were Jatizso and Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hey! Who's telling this story?

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Ahem. At the time it was customary for the Kings' sons to claim a prize from his treasury when they came of age.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Each brother rallied support and strode out to confront the other on the field of battle to claim the same prize.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: The brothers met on the field, limbs were bruised, bones were crushed and heads clashed.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: They probably didn't notice the head-clashing bit. Too thick skulled!

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Can I finish the story without interruptions?

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Carry on dear.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Anyway, long into the night they fought, until they stood alone, facing each other.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: On the second day the old king, whose patience was exhausted, strode out onto the field and demanded to know who had claimed his prize.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso looked at his father and told him yes, it was his, to which the other called out 'No! It's not'.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: And so, the argument continued through to the third day, with the sounds of 'Jah! 'tis so!', 'Ne! It's not!' echoing over the field, becoming more hoarse as the darkness enveloped the brothers.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Bah! What they needed was a good clip round the ear.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: At the end of the third day, when the brothers lay unable to move, let alone stand, the King stood between them in shame.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: As punishment for their squabbling he banished his sons from his realm, ordering that neither should return until they had conquered lands of their own.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso set sail with a band of loyal followers, sailing into the east, while Neitiznot went west.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso ventured far into uncharted oceans, his crew dwindling as the trials of sailing and sea monsters took them.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso's ship limped through the frozen wastes, in need of repairs, fighting off pirates who thought them easy pickings.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: At last a promising land was sighted, a land rich in minerals, deep fjords and sweeping mountains.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso claimed the land and named it after himself.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: A mighty citadel was constructed to protect his land; then Jatizso set forth to explore his new kingdom.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Crossing a few fjords, Jatizso spied an island which remained green and fertile, even at such latitude.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: With a mighty cheer Jatizso and his men marched on the island. Upon reaching the island, however, they discovered a wooden stockade.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Determined to be master of all the land, Jatizso stood in front of the gates. He looked up and demanded that those within the stockade surrendered.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso was horrified when his brother appeared on the battlements and cried 'This is my land! Go find yer own'. To which his brother replied 'No it's not!', throwing some rocks for good measure.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: And so the blood feud continued.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: The fighting between the towns has continued for generations with little sign of improvement.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Quite recently a massive troll army seized one of the islands to the north, while our townships' militias were busy fighting one another.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: They marched down and razed Neitiznot to the ground while Neitiznot's troops laid siege to Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: The smoke damage was atrocious, I had to replace my entire wardrobe.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Yes, dear.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Anyway, a hasty sort of peace has been agreed until the trolls are dealt with.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: But you can still see the bitter rivalry as guards hurl insults between the towns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the King of Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Ha! Our benevolent King Gjuki Sorvott IV.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: He is short-tempered, arrogant and cares little about anything except outdoing Neitiznot's Burgher.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: He's not at all like Burgher Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I'm surprised King Sorvott wishes to talk to you. Don't expect much in the way of gratitude.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you ferry me to Jatizso?

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too busy to help your King.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I guess I won't be getting paid for finding you, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be back later.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Bye!

sections.title: After the Fremennik Isles on Jatizso

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hi, [Fremennik name], you should speak with King Sorvott in the main chambers.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: The chambers have the town's banner erected outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for bringing me here Mord.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso is a small Fremennik town, which is far less closed to the world than other Fremennik tribes.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: We maintain trade links with many powers in the world and have moved beyond some traditions to take advantage of modern advances.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: We do, however, continue to 'enlighten' our neighbours in Neitiznot. Unfortunately, some of the Islands are infested with trolls.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: We should really be trying to deal with the trolls, but the King tends to get a bit preoccupied with his cousin, the Burgher of Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too busy to help your King.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I guess I won't be getting paid for finding you, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be back later.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the King of Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Ha! Our benevolent King Gjuki Sorvott IV. He doesn't have much of an opinion about anyone, apart from his cousin Mawnis who rules the other Island. He hates him with a passion that can occasionally be quite scary.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: You might be able to talk to him if you help out around Jatizso. Don't expect much in the way of gratitude though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you ferry me to Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm fine right now.
